# Protected paths, longwalk.
#
# The single source of truth for which paths require a signed consensus
# decision record (docs/decisions/NNN-*.md, signed by every worker the record
# names as dispatched) before a PR touching them can merge. Read by
# tools/check_consensus.py, enforced by .github/workflows/consensus.yml.
#
# Format: one path per line. Blank lines and lines starting with '#' are
# ignored. A line ending in '/' matches that directory and everything under
# it. Any other line matches that exact file path. Paths are repo-relative,
# forward-slash separated (the repo is cross-platform-clean, see CLAUDE.md).
#
# What belongs here: paths where a unilateral change by one resident would be
# expensive or invisible to undo. Not "important files". Plenty of important
# code is not listed here because a bad change to it shows up as a failing
# test, which is a cheaper gate than a consensus record.

# Engine and project configuration. A change here can alter the behavior of
# every scene at once, and engine changes are a Scott escalation anyway.
project.godot
export_presets.cfg
tools/godot/VERSION

# The architecture writeup. Architecture changes are a Scott escalation; this
# entry makes an attempt to edit the design visible at the gate.
ARCHITECTURE.md

# The constitution and its generated twin. Constitution edits are a Scott
# escalation. AGENTS.md is generated from CLAUDE.md by
# tools/generate_agents_md.sh, so it moves only when CLAUDE.md does.
CLAUDE.md
AGENTS.md

# Sim-layer contracts. CLAUDE.md's simulation/rendering separation makes this
# module tree the boundary the whole server-backend evolution depends on: sim
# code has zero viewport/camera/UI dependencies and runs headless. Enumerated
# as a directory rather than file-by-file so new sim contracts are protected
# the day they land rather than the day someone remembers to add them here.
#
# Current contents: game_state.gd (session carrier, autoloaded as GameState)
# and town_layout.gd (authored starter-town data, class_name TownLayout).
#
# src/legacy_procedural/sim/ is deliberately NOT protected: it is parked, not
# wired into the active project, and gated only by
# tools/run_legacy_procedural_tests.sh.
src/sim/

# The team framework itself. Role briefs are injected at dispatch time and
# shape how every resident behaves, so a quiet edit to one is a quiet edit to
# the team. Same for this file: an unreviewed removal of a protected path is
# how a protection silently stops existing.
roles/
.github/protected-paths.txt
